error.regex: (device descriptor read.*error|usb.*descriptor.*failed|USB.*EPROTO|Cannot enumerate USB|device not accepting address)

verdict.summary: Linux fails to enumerate USB device. Error -71 (EPROTO) = protocol failure during descriptor read. Hardware/signal issue.

dead_ends.action: Recompile kernel with different USB options
dead_ends.why_fails: Protocol errors during enumeration are electrical issues, not kernel config
USB 3.0 cables may lack proper USB 2.0 data pair wiring. Use known-good USB 2.0 cable.
Autosuspend affects already-enumerated devices, not initial enumeration

workarounds.action: Try a different USB port directly on the motherboard
workarounds.how: Front panel ports have longer wires and more EMI. Use rear motherboard ports.

workarounds.action: Use a powered USB hub for adequate power delivery
Check dmesg for specific error code
